reasoning: The stock experienced a rapid jump in late February from ~$110 to $115.82, diverging heavily from its linear trend. The time-series statistical model provides a combined probability of 0.39 for exceeding this peak by March 9, factoring in mean reversion and seasonality. Furthermore, a $0.52 ex-dividend date falls immediately after the forecasting date (March 3), mechanically placing downward pressure on the stock price in the near term. These factors give a strong indication that the stock will struggle to break the $115.82 threshold in the immediate weeks. Over longer time horizons, the initial high-water mark becomes less of a relative hurdle as the equity risk premium pushes the value upwards, pulling probabilities back toward a neutral 0.50 in compliance with the instruction to increase uncertainty over long horizons.
